---
title: "Internal Ballistics"
subtitle: "0D Modeling of Chamber Pressure and Thrust-Time Evolution"
order: 5
---

## Introduction

Having defined propellant properties (Chapter 2), grain geometry (Chapter 3), and how the burning surface evolves with web distance (Chapter 4 — burnback), we now combine these to predict the time-dependent behavior of a solid rocket motor.

This chapter introduces the **zero-dimensional (0D) internal ballistics model** — the standard engineering approach for preliminary design and performance prediction. It assumes spatially uniform pressure and temperature inside the combustion chamber.

## Mass Balance in the Combustion Chamber

The foundation is conservation of mass:

$$
\frac{dm_c}{dt} = \dot{m}_\text{gen} - \dot{m}_\text{out}
$$

where:
- \( m_c \): mass of combustion products in the chamber (kg)
- \( \dot{m}_\text{gen} = \rho_p A_b(t) r(P_c) \): mass generation rate from propellant burning
- \( \dot{m}_\text{out} \): mass expulsion rate through the nozzle
- \( \rho_p \): propellant density (kg/m³)
- \( A_b(t) \): instantaneous burning surface area (m²) from burnback
- \( r(P_c) \): linear burn rate (m/s)

## Burn Rate Law (Vieille’s Law)

The propellant regression rate is described by:

$$
r(P_c) = a P_c^n
$$

where:
- \( a \): burn rate coefficient
- \( n \): pressure exponent (typically 0.2–0.7 for stable motors)
- \( P_c \): chamber pressure

A temperature-sensitive form is often used:

$$
a(T_i) = a_\text{ref} \left[1 + \sigma_p (T_i - T_\text{ref})\right]
$$

## Nozzle Mass Flow Rate

For choked nozzle flow:

$$
\dot{m}_\text{out} = \frac{P_c A_t}{c^*}
$$

where \( A_t \) is the throat area and \( c^* \) is the characteristic velocity of the propellant.

## Governing Differential Equation

Substituting and simplifying yields the core 0D ODE for chamber pressure:

$$
\frac{V_c}{c^{*2}} \frac{dP_c}{dt} = \rho_p A_b(w) r(P_c) - \frac{P_c A_t}{c^*}
$$

In practice we solve the coupled system for \( P_c(t) \) and web distance \( w(t) \), with \( A_b(w) \) supplied by the burnback module and numerical integration (e.g., `scipy.integrate.solve_ivp`).

## Key Assumptions

- Uniform chamber pressure and temperature (lumped parameter)
- Constant propellant density and \( c^* \)
- Negligible erosive burning and ignition transients (added in later chapters)

## Summary

This chapter establishes the fundamental differential equations linking grain regression to motor performance. The next chapter explores the K/N ratio and equilibrium pressure in greater depth.

## References

- Sutton, G. P., & Biblarz, O. (2017). *Rocket Propulsion Elements* (9th ed.). Wiley.
- Greatrix, D. R. (2012). *Powered Flight – The Engineering of Aerospace Propulsion*. Springer.
- Zucrow, M. J., & Hoffman, J. D. (1976). *Gas Dynamics* (Vol. 1). Wiley.
- Wimpress, R. N. (1950). *Internal Ballistics of Solid-Fuel Rockets*. McGraw-Hill.

:::{.callout-note}
Executable Python implementations and example simulations are provided in the `code/` directory.
:::
